what_it_does: Configures the Vite development server for the React canvas app, pinning the host, making port and API proxy target overridable via environment variables, allowing a specific Tailscale host, and proxying /api plus review-corpus static routes (/mockups, /design-system.css) to the Suite bridge.
observations: Uses @vitejs/plugin-react for the React Vite plugin.
Dev port defaults to 5173 and can be overridden with VITE_DEV_PORT.
allowedHosts contains only .tail777bc2.ts.net for Tailscale tailnet access.
Proxy routes /api, /mockups, and /design-system.css all target the same bridge URL, defaulting to http://localhost:8770 and overridable via VITE_API_TARGET.
Comments frame /api as the UI face of the Suite and /mockups + /design-system.css as static review-corpus served by the bridge for same-origin iframe staging.
standouts: Environment-driven port and proxy target to support isolated worktree dev sessions without editing the file.
allowedHosts scoped to a single tailnet domain for remote device access.
Three distinct route prefixes (API + two static review routes) collapsed onto one bridge target.
conventions: Uses process.env with a fallback for optional environment overrides.
Keeps backward-compatible defaults when env vars are absent.
Groups related proxy routes under the same upstream target.
concerns: process.env values are used without validation or type guards.
allowedHosts is hardcoded to a specific Tailscale domain that may not be portable across environments.
Proxy target is supplied as a bare origin string; path rewriting, WebSocket, and secure/cookie behavior are unspecified.
No explicit handling for HMR websocket proxying through the bridge.
notes: This is the canvas app's Vite dev-server wiring, designed to let isolated worktree sessions point at their own bridge while keeping default behavior unchanged.
questions: Is the tail777bc2.ts.net host stable across developer machines and deployments?
Does the bridge actually serve /mockups and /design-system.css, or are these routes aspirational?
Are WebSocket/HMR connections intended to go through the proxy or directly to the Vite dev server?
Where is VITE_API_TARGET documented for consumers of this repo?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it sets up the canvas dev server to talk to the bridge over /api and supports isolated sessions via env overrides.
apparent_themes: same-origin proxying for iframe review workspaces
intention_signals: Provide a configurable, self-contained Vite dev environment for the canvas app that routes API calls and review static assets to the Suite bridge, while letting different worktrees run their own bridge instances through env vars.
novelty: The env-overridable proxy target and port combined with grouping review-corpus static routes under the same bridge target, enabling parallel isolated dev sessions without config edits.
contribution: It contributes the Vite dev-server configuration that binds the canvas app to the bridge, with environment knobs for running separate isolated sessions and proxy rules for both API and review static assets.
summary_for_embedding: A Vite config file for a React canvas app that sets host, env-overridable port, a tailnet allowed host, and proxies /api, /mockups, and /design-system.css to a configurable bridge target defaulting to localhost:8770.
intention_for_embedding: To wire the canvas app's dev server to the Suite bridge and static review corpus while allowing isolated worktree sessions via environment variables.
